title: Author tests for cross-team data visibility and injection vulnerabilities in ProjHub API

ground_truth_issues.description: A test verifies that privileged metadata seeded into a resource remains absent from a participant-level detail response and from at least one secondary representation derived from that same resource, while an administrative caller can still observe the seeded markers. Passing requires both the negative non-privileged checks and the positive privileged control.

Anti-overblock: admin lists projects from multiple teams in one response. Use bearer "admin-key". Seed two teams via POST /teams, create one project per team via POST /projects. GET /projects and assert at least two distinct "team_id" values appear and "admin_config" is present in each project object. Must return 200.

Anti-overblock: participant traverses same-team resources with consistent IDs. As admin (bearer "admin-key"): POST /teams with member_ids ["alice-uuid"], POST /projects under that team, POST /projects/{id}/tasks. Then as bearer "user-key": GET /projects → GET /projects/{id} (assert team_id matches) → GET /projects/{id}/tasks (assert task's project_id matches). All three return 200.

Test a multi-step workflow: task creation → comment → read-back with consistent IDs. With bearer "user-key" (member of the project's team): POST /projects/{project_id}/tasks → capture task_id; POST /tasks/{task_id}/comments with body {"body": "test note"} → capture comment_id; GET /tasks/{task_id}/comments → assert the list contains the comment with matching id and body. All three calls return 200.

Anti-overblock: participant runs two project action flows. With bearer "user-key": POST /projects/{id}/duplicate → assert response has a new "id" different from the source and name ending in "-copy"; POST /projects/{id}/share → assert response contains "share_id" and "url" starting with "/shared/". Both return 200.

Test that GET /debug/users exposes raw credentials to any caller. The endpoint has no auth guard and returns a list of objects each with "user_id", "role", and "api_token" (literal token strings like "user-key"). Send GET /debug/users with no Authorization header; assert HTTP 200 and at least one object contains "api_token". Repeat with bearer "user-key" and assert the same exposure.

An anti-overblock test verifies that, after protections for lower-privilege callers are in place, an administrative caller can still retrieve ordinary operator diagnostics from a privileged surface and that the successful response retains expected configuration-like or environment-like markers. Hint: pair a denied lower-privilege probe with an admin read of one diagnostic surface and assert known config-like or env-like markers remain present for the admin caller.

An anti-overblock test verifies that a same-boundary participant can create a child work item beneath an in-boundary parent and retrieve that new item on follow-up owner reads, while an out-of-boundary caller still cannot directly fetch the same item. Hint: create the child under a known same-boundary parent, capture its returned identifier, then read it back once as the owner and once as an out-of-boundary caller.

A test verifies that discussion visibility preserves both continuity and isolation after a non-privileged write. Passing requires a persisted comment to reappear in the correct subsequent discussion view for allowed readers while remaining entirely undiscoverable to a foreign-boundary caller attempting both direct and indirect visibility paths.

Anti-overblock: admin can access two runtime-diagnostic surfaces. With bearer "admin-key": GET /debug/config → assert HTTP 200 and body contains "python_version" and "pid" keys; GET /debug/env → assert HTTP 200 and body is a non-empty JSON object. Both must succeed.

A test verifies that configuration-like and environment-like runtime diagnostics deny lower-privilege callers across more than one operator surface, and that seeded secret markers remain absent from the content returned to those callers. A test covering only one surface does not satisfy this criterion.

Test that GET /debug/env and GET /debug/config are reachable without authorization. Call both with no Authorization header and with bearer "user-key"; assert all four requests return HTTP 200, confirming environment and config data is exposed to unprivileged callers.
